name: rotateLeft
description:
Rebalances tree as described below

X                                           Y
/ \                                         / \
Y   c         right rotate -->              a   X
/ \            <--  left rotate                 / \
a   b                                           b   c

name: rotateRight
description: Rebalances tree as described in rotateLeft

name: src/public/iterators.js
BaseIterator
description:
Base class for STL-like iterators. It references a node (or index) and a container.
Navigation is achieved by calling container's prev() and next() methods.

name: Iterator
description: STL-like forward iterator. It's more verbose than ES6 iterators, but allows iteration over any part of the container

name: constructor
description:
There are 3 ways to construct an iterator:

1. Using a node and a container
2. Copy constructor / clone
3. Copy constructor / clone from ReverseIterator instance

name: next
description:
Replaces node reference with the reference of the next node in the container.
Can be used for manual iteration over a range of key-value pairs.

name: __n
description: __n and __c are defined in the base class

name: prev
description:
Replaces node reference with the reference of the previous node in the container
Can be used for manual reverse iteration over a range of key-value pairs.

name: ReverseIterator
description:
STL-like backward iterator. Can be used to traverse container or a range in the reverse order.
It's more verbose than ES6 iterators, but allows iteration over any part of the container

name: constructor
description:
There are 3 ways to construct a reverse iterator:

1. Using a node and a container
2. Copy constructor / clone
3. Copy constructor / clone from forward Iterator instance

params.name: args
params.description: One of possible sets of constructor parameters

name: next
description:
Replaces node reference with the reference of the previous node in the container, because it works in reverse order
Can be used for manual reverse iteration over a range of key-value pairs.

name: __n
description: __n and __c are defined in the base class

name: prev
description:
Replaces node reference with the reference of the next node in the container, because it works in reverse order
Can be used for manual forward iteration over a range of key-value pairs.

name: src/public/main.js
src/public/tree-map.js
TreeMap
description:
TreeMap is an associative container that stores elements formed
by a combination of a key value and a mapped value, following a specific order.

In a TreeMap, the key values are generally used to sort and uniquely identify
the elements, while the mapped values store the content associated to this key.
The types of key and mapped value may differ.

## Container properties
**Associative** - Elements in associative containers are referenced by their key
and not by their absolute position in the container.
**Ordered** - The elements in the container follow a strict order at all times.
All inserted elements are given a position in this order.
**Map** - Each element associates a key to a mapped value. Keys are meant
to identify the elements whose main content is the mapped value.
**Unique keys** - No two elements in the container can have equivalent keys.

name: constructor
description: Creates an empty, or a pre-initialized map.

params.name: iterable
params.description: Another iterable object whose key-value pairs are added into the newly created map.

name: __t
description: Internal tree

description: Returns class name of this object
examples: Object.prototype.toString.call(new TreeMap()); // "[object TreeMap]"

unknown.tagValue: {string} class name

return.description: class name

description: Allows to create programmatically an instance of the same class

unknown.tagValue: {object} constructor object for this class.

return.description: constructor object for this class.

name: clear
description: Removes all key-value pairs.

name: delete
description: Removes key-value pair with the specified key if such entry exists. Does nothing otherwise.

name: entries
description: Forward ES6 iterator for all key-value pairs in ascending order of the keys.

unknown.tagValue: {JsIterator} forward iterator for all nodes in the container

return.description: forward iterator for all nodes in the container

name: forEach
description:
Iterates all key-value pairs using a callback in ascending order of the keys.
Note that ES6 specifies the order of key value parameters in the callback differently from for-of loop.

params.name: callback
params.description: similar to forEach callbacks for standard JS maps

name: get
description: Finds value associated with the specified key. If specified key does not exist then undefined is returned.

unknown.tagValue: {any} the value associated with the given key

params.name: key
params.description: a value of any type that can be compared with a key

return.description: the value associated with the given key

name: has
description: A boolean indicator whether map contains a key-value pair with the specified key
examples:
let map = new TreeMap([[1, 'A'], [2, 'B'], [3, 'C']]);
let b = map.get(3); // true

unknown.tagValue: {boolean} returns `true` if given key exists in the container

params.name: key
params.description: a value of any type that can be compared with a key

return.description: returns `true` if given key exists in the container

description: Forward ES6 iterator for all keys in ascending order of the keys.

unknown.tagValue: {JsIterator} forward iterator

return.description: forward iterator

name: set
description: Adds or updates key-value pair to the map.
examples:
let map = new TreeMap();
map.set(1, 'A');

params.name: key
params.description: Key for the node to be added/updated

name: size
description: Number of key-value pairs in the map.

unknown.tagValue: {number} - number of elements in the container

return.description: number of elements in the container

name: values
description: Forward ES6 iterator for all values in ascending order of the keys.

unknown.tagValue: {JsIterator} forward iterator for all values

return.description: forward iterator for all values

name: [Symbol.iterator]
description: Forward ES6 iterator for all key-value pairs in ascending order of the keys. The same as entries() method

unknown.tagValue: {JsIterator} forward iterator for all elements of the container

return.description: forward iterator for all elements of the container

name: backward
description: ES6 reverse iterator for all key-value pairs in descending order of the keys.

unknown.tagValue: {JsReverseIterator} reverse iterator for all elements in the container

return.description: reverse iterator for all elements in the container

name: compareFunc
description:
Sets custom comparison function if key values are not of primitive types.
Callback is a 3-way comparison function accepts two key values (lhs, rhs). It is expected to return
   +1 if the value of rhs is greater than lhs
   -1 if the value of rhs is less than lhs
    0 if values are the same

name: src/public/tree-multimap.js
TreeMultiMap
description:
TreeMultiMap is an associative container that stores elements formed by
a combination of a key value and a mapped value, following a specific order,
and where multiple elements can have equivalent keys.

In a TreeMultiMap, the key values are generally used to sort and uniquely
identify the elements, while the mapped values store the content
associated to this key. The types of key and mapped value may differ.

## Container properties
**Associative** - Elements in associative containers are referenced
by their key and not by their absolute position in the container.
**Ordered** - The elements in the container follow a strict order
at all times. All inserted elements are given a position in this order.
**Map** - Each element associates a key to a mapped value. Keys are meant
to identify the elements whose main content is the mapped value.
**Multiple equivalent keys** - Multiple elements in the container
can have equivalent keys.

name: src/public/tree-multiset.js
TreeMultiSet
description:
TreeMultiSet is a container that stores elements following a specific order,
and where multiple elements can have equivalent values.

In a TreeMultiSet, the value of an element also identifies it
(the value is itself the key). The value of the elements in a multiset
cannot be modified once in the container (the elements are always immutable),
but they can be inserted or removed from the container.

## Container properties
**Associative** - Elements in associative containers are referenced
by their key and not by their absolute position in the container.
**Ordered** - The elements in the container follow a strict order
at all times. All inserted elements are given a position in this order.
**Set** - The value of an element is also the key used to identify it.
**Multiple equivalent keys** - Multiple elements in the container
can have equivalent keys.

name: constructor
description: Creates an empty, or a pre-initialized set.

params.name: iterable
params.description: Another iterable object whose values are added into the newly created set.

name: __t
description: Internal tree

name: [Symbol.toStringTag]
description: String tag of this class
examples: Object.prototype.toString.call(new TreeMultiSet()); // "[object TreeMultiSet]"

unknown.tagValue: {string} class name

return.description: class name

name: [Symbol.species]
description: Allows to create programmatically an instance of the same class

unknown.tagValue: {any} constructor object for this class.

return.description: constructor object for this class.

name: clear
description: Removes all key-value pairs.

name: delete
description: Removes key-value pair with the specified key if such entry exists. Does nothing otherwise.

name: entries
description: Forward ES6 iterator for all values in ascending order.

unknown.tagValue: {JsIterator} forward iterator for all elements

return.description: forward iterator for all elements

name: forEach
description:
Iterates all values using a callback in ascending order.
Note that ES6 specifies the order of key parameters in the callback differently from for-of loop.

params.name: callback
params.description: The same type of callback as used in `forEach` of standard JS maps

name: has
description: A boolean indicator whether set contains the specified key.

unknown.tagValue: {boolean} `true` when key exists in the container

params.name: key
params.description: a value of any type that can be compared with a key

return.description: `true` when key exists in the container

name: keys
description: Forward ES6 iterator for all keys in ascending order.

unknown.tagValue: {JsIterator} forward iterator for all keys

return.description: forward iterator for all keys

name: add
description: Adds a key to the set. If the key already exists then another entry with the same value is added.

name: size
description: Number of keys in the set.

unknown.tagValue: {number} count of elements in the container

return.description: count of elements in the container

name: values
description: Forward ES6 iterator for all keys in ascending order. It is the same as keys() method

unknown.tagValue: {JsIterator} forward iterator for all values

return.description: forward iterator for all values

name: [Symbol.iterator]
description: Forward ES6 iterator for all keys in ascending order. The same as entries() method

unknown.tagValue: {JsIterator} forward iterator for all elements

return.description: forward iterator for all elements

name: backward
description: ES6 reverse iterator for all keys in descending order.

unknown.tagValue: {JsReverseIterator} reverse iterator for all elements

return.description: reverse iterator for all elements

name: compareFunc
description:
Sets custom comparison function if key values are not of primitive types.
Callback is a 3-way comparison function accepts two key values (lhs, rhs). It is expected to return
     +1 if the value of rhs is greater than lhs
     -1 if the value of rhs is less than lhs
      0 if values are the same

name: src/public/tree-set.js
TreeSet
description: TreeSet is a container that stores unique elements following a specific order. In a TreeSet, the value of an element also identifies it (the value is itself the key), and each value must be unique. The value of the elements in a TreeSet cannot be modified once in the container (the elements are immutable), but they can be inserted or removed from the container. ## Container properties **Associative** - Elements in associative containers are referenced by their key and not by their absolute position in the container. **Ordered** - The elements in the container follow a strict order at all times. All inserted elements are given a position in this order. **Set** - The value of an element is also the key used to identify it. **Unique keys** - No two elements in the container can have equivalent keys.

name: ./README.md

content:
{
  "name": "jstreemap",
  "type": "commonjs",
  "version": "2.0.0",
  "description": "Library of associative containers; it implements TreeMap, TreeSet, TreeMultiMap and TreeMultiSet classes",
  "main": "jstreemap.js",
  "files": [
    "/jstreemap.js",
    "/index.d.ts"
  ],
  "scripts": {
    "analyze-tests": "node ./tools/analyze-tests.js dev-tests.xml",
    "esdoc": "esdoc -c ./esdoc.json",
    "eslint-fix": "eslint --config=./eslint.config.js --fix src/**/*.js test/**/*.js gulpfile.js",
    "eslint": "eslint --config=./eslint.config.js src/**/*.js test/**/*.js gulpfile.js",
    "test-watch": "cross-env DEV_TEST=true mocha --reporter spec -w --recursive test/specs/*.spec.js",
    "dev-test": "cross-env DEV_TEST=true nyc --reporter=html --reporter=text --report-dir=./build/coverage --check-coverage --lines 100 --functions 100 --branches 100 mocha --reporter mocha-multi-reporters --reporter-options configFile=./config/mocha-reporters.json test/specs/*.spec.js",
    "prod-test": "mocha --reporter spec test/specs/*.spec.js",
    "web-test": "mocha --reporter spec test/browser/*.spec.js",
    "webpack": "webpack --config ./config/webpack.config.js"
  },
  "directories": {
    "test": "test"
  },
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "git+https://github.com/kirusi/jstreemap.git"
  },
  "keywords": [
    "set",
    "map",
    "multimap",
    "multiset",
    "red",
    "black",
    "tree",
    "stl",
    "iterator",
    "multimap",
    "treemap",
    "multiset",
    "treeset",
    "lower_bound",
    "lowerBound",
    "upper_bound",
    "upperBound",
    "ordered",
    "container",
    "find",
    "insert",
    "delete",
    "erase",
    "template",
    "library",
    "dictionary",
    "key",
    "value"
  ],
  "author": "Kirusi Msafiri",
  "license": "0BSD",
  "bugs": {
    "url": "https://github.com/kirusi/jstreemap/issues"
  },
  "homepage": "https://github.com/kirusi/jstreemap#readme",
  "devDependencies": {
    "cross-env": "^10.1.0",
    "del": "^8.0.1",
    "esdoc": "^1.1.0",
    "esdoc-node": "^1.0.5",
    "esdoc-standard-plugin": "^1.0.0",
    "eslint": "^9.39.4",
    "gulp": "^4.0.2",
    "lighthouse-badges": "^1.5.4",
    "mocha": "^11.7.6",
    "mocha-junit-reporter": "^2.2.1",
    "mocha-lcov-reporter": "^1.3.0",
    "mocha-multi-reporters": "^1.5.1",
    "nyc": "^18.0.0",
    "plugin-error": "^1.0.1",
    "selenium-webdriver": "^4.44.0",
    "should": "^13.2.3",
    "webpack": "^5.107.2",
    "webpack-cli": "^7.0.3"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "eslint-config-prettier": "^10.1.8",
    "eslint-plugin-import": "^2.32.0",
    "eslint-plugin-jsdoc": "^63.0.2",
    "eslint-plugin-prettier": "^5.5.6",
    "typescript-eslint": "^8.61.0"
  }
}
name: package.json
